title: elymus virginicus f. australis
intro: Elymus virginicus f. australis, also known as the Virginia wild rye or tall wild rye, is a subspecies of a perennial grass native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial in treating wounds and reducing fever. The health benefits of Elymus virginicus f. australis include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for use in medicinal applications, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
